---
title: "FDR4VGT Atmospheric Correction"
subtitle: "Algorithm Theoretical Basis Document (ATBD)"
version: "1.1"
reference: FDR4VGT-AC-ATBD
client: C3S
contrat: FDR4VGT
date: today
author:
  - "HYGEOS"

format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    template: _extensions/hygeos/resources/templates/hygeos-template.tex

toc: true
number-sections: true
fontsize: 11pt
---

<!-- Ajout d'une table des matières -->

\tableofcontents
{{< pagebreak >}}

<!-- Ajout d'une table des figures et des tableaux -->

\listoftables
{{< pagebreak >}}

## Version History

| Version | Date       | Author(s) | Description |
|---------|------------|-----------|-------------|
| v1.0    | 2024-01-XX | HYGEOS    | Initial ATBD for FDR4VGT atmospheric correction |
| v1.1    | 2025-07-17 | HYGEOS    | Implementation notes, performance optimizations, float32 fix, known limitations |
| v1.1    | 2026-07-17 | HYGEOS    | Addendum: Appendix E with SMACCL OpenCL kernel and wrapper API contract |

**Contributors:** HYGEOS S.A., Brussels, Belgium

## Acronyms

| Acronym | Definition |
|---------|------------|
| AC      | Atmospheric Correction |
| AERONET | AErosol RObotic NETwork |
| AOD     | Aerosol Optical Depth |
| AMIP    | Atmospheric Model Intercomparison Project |
| ARTDECO | Atmospheric Radiative Transfer Data Centre |
| BRDF    | Bidirectional Reflectance Distribution Function |
| CAMS    | Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service |
| C3S     | Copernicus Climate Change Service |
| CF      | Climate and Forecast (metadata conventions) |
| CTM     | Chemical Transport Model |
| DEM     | Digital Elevation Model |
| DTOA-r  | Error on TOA reflectance |
| FDR     | Full Dynamic Range |
| FIDUCEO | Fiducial Data for Earth Observation |
| GPU     | Graphics Processing Unit |
| HITRAN  | High-Resolution Transmission Molecular Database |
| L1C     | Level 1C (TOA reflectance product) |
| L2A     | Level 2A (TOC reflectance product) |
| LUT     | Look-Up Table |
| MERRA-2 | Modern-Era Retrospective analysis for Research and Applications, Version 2 |
| MLP     | Multi-Layer Perceptron |
| NA      | Not Applicable |
| ONNX    | Open Neural Network Exchange |
| OPAC    | Optical Properties of Aerosols and Clouds |
| PDF     | Probability Density Function |
| PSF     | Point Spread Function |
| RAA     | Relative Azimuth Angle |
| RMSE    | Root Mean Square Error |
| RTM     | Radiative Transfer Model |
| RSRF    | Relative Spectral Response Function |
| SMAC    | Simplified Method for Atmospheric Correction |
| SMAC-CL | SMAC implementation in OpenCL |
| SZA     | Solar Zenith Angle |
| TOA     | Top of Atmosphere |
| TOC     | Top of Canopy |
| UH₂O    | Total Column Water Vapor |
| UO₃     | Total Column Ozone |
| VGT     | Vegetation Monitoring Tool (SPOT) |
| VZA     | View Zenith Angle |
| VAA     | View Azimuth Angle |

{{< pagebreak >}}

# Introduction

This Algorithm Theoretical Basis Document (ATBD) describes the atmospheric correction (AC) algorithm for the Full Dynamic Range (FDR) Vegetation Monitoring Tool (VGT) product, including both SPOT-VGT and Proba-V sensors. The algorithm implements the Simplified Method for Atmospheric Correction (SMAC) with rigorous uncertainty propagation following the FIDUCEO (Fiducial Data for Earth Observation) methodology.

**Version 1.1 updates:**

- Documents all performance optimizations implemented in the production codebase (tile processing, Zarr caching, local I/O, COW shared state)
- Specifies float32 data type requirement for SLP/T10M fields to prevent float16 overflow in pressure computation
- Updates DEM interpolation note (linear interpolation improvement over nearest-neighbor)
- Documents Köppen-Geiger regionalization as a planned feature
- Confirms adjacency effects as a known limitation
- Adds implementation-specific details (tile grid, ONNX model inputs, quality flag bits, output NetCDF variables)
- Adds the SMACCL OpenCL kernel and Python wrapper API contract (Appendix E)

The FDR4VGT AC algorithm provides:

- **Top-of-Canopy (TOC) directional reflectance** for 4 spectral bands (Blue, Red, NIR, SWIR)
- **Complete uncertainty budget** with propagation of all major uncertainty sources
- **Quality flags** for cloud contamination, high AOD, high solar zenith angle, and AOD gradients
- **Jacobian matrices** for sensitivity analysis of TOC reflectance to input parameters

# Input and Auxiliary Data

## Level-1C Input

The input data is the Level-1C (L1C) FDR product, which provides TOA reflectance at the sensor spectral bands with associated geometry and quality information.

**Supported sensors:**

| Sensor | Bands | Spatial Resolution |
|--------|-------|-------------------|
| SPOT-VGT | B1 (Blue), B2 (Red), B3 (NIR), B4 (SWIR) | ~1 km (nadir) |
| Proba-V | B1 (Blue), B2 (Red), B3 (NIR), B4 (SWIR) | ~1 km (nadir) |

: Supported sensors and bands {#tbl-sensors}

**Required input variables:**

| Variable | Description | Dimensions |
|----------|-------------|------------|
| `TOA` | TOA reflectance | (bands, y, x) |
| `DTOA-r` | TOA reflectance error | (bands, y, x) |
| `SZA` | Solar Zenith Angle | (y, x) |
| `SAA` | Solar Azimuth Angle | (y, x) |
| `VZA` | View Zenith Angle (VNIR) | (y, x) |
| `VAA` | View Azimuth Angle | (y, x) |
| `VZA_IR` | View Zenith Angle (SWIR, Proba-V) | (y, x) |
| `VAA_IR` | View Azimuth Angle (SWIR, Proba-V) | (y, x) |
| `clm` | Cloud mask | (y, x) |
| `lat` | Latitude | (y, x) |
| `lon` | Longitude | (y, x) |

: Required L1C input variables {#tbl-l1c-vars}

**Notes:**

- Proba-V has a dual-FOV sensor (VNIR and SWIR with different viewing geometries), hence separate `VZA_IR`/`VAA_IR` for the SWIR band
- Cloud mask (`clm`) values: 0 = clear, non-zero = cloud-contaminated
- Sensor readers use `core.interpolate` for resampling (generic xarray-based interpolation)

## Digital Elevation Model

The Digital Elevation Model (DEM) provides terrain elevation for surface pressure correction and terrain effect estimation.

**Source:** GTOPO30 (90m global resolution)

**Fields:**

| Field | Description | Units |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| `elev` | Terrain elevation | meters |
| `Delev` | Elevation difference (for uncertainty) | meters |

: DEM fields {#tbl-dem-fields}

**Interpolation:** Linear interpolation to satellite grid

> **Implementation Note (v1.1):** The implementation uses linear interpolation (via `scipy.interpolate.RegularGridInterpolator`) rather than the nearest-neighbor method specified in ATBD v1.0. This is a **reasonable improvement** — linear interpolation provides smoother elevation transitions at tile boundaries. The impact is negligible for the 90m GTOPO30 resolution vs ~1km VGT pixels.

**DEM Cropping (v1.1):** The global GTOPO30 DEM is cropped to the product's lat/lon extent before loading, reducing memory from ~1.9 GB to ~0.2 GB for typical narrow longitude ranges.

## MERRA-2 Auxiliary Data

MERRA-2 (Modern-Era Retrospective analysis for Research and Applications, Version 2) provides atmospheric state variables for atmospheric correction.

**Source:** MERRA-2 instantaneous 2D aerosol and meteorological fields

**Required fields:**

| Variable | Description | MERRA-2 Unit | SMAC Unit | Conversion |
|----------|-------------|--------------|-----------|------------|
| `TO3` | Total ozone column | Dobson | cm.atm | ×1e-3 |
| `TQV` | Total column water vapor | kg.m⁻² | g.cm⁻² | ×1e-1 |
| `SLP` | Sea-level pressure | Pa | hPa | ×1e-2 |
| `T10M` | Temperature at 10m | K | K | — |
| `TOTEXTTAU` | Total AOD at 550nm | — | — | — |

: MERRA-2 fields and unit conversions {#tbl-merra2-fields}

**Aerosol species fractions:**

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `SU_FRAC` | Sulfate fraction |
| `DU_FRAC` | Dust fraction |
| `OC_FRAC` | Organic carbon fraction |
| `SS_FRAC` | Sea salt fraction |
| `BC_FRAC` | Black carbon fraction |

: Aerosol species fractions {#tbl-aerosol-species}

**Interpolation:** `scipy.interpolate.RegularGridInterpolator` with `method='linear'` (mathematically equivalent to bilinear interpolation for regular grids). Temporal interpolation includes time as a third dimension (3D interpolation: time, lat, lon).

> **Implementation Note (v1.1):** The implementation pre-interpolates MERRA-2 fields to the full satellite grid once and stores them in a local Zarr cache (`precompute_ancillary()`). Tiles are then sliced from the local cache. This is numerically equivalent to per-tile interpolation but provides significant I/O performance improvement (see @sec-perf-opt).

> **Critical Data Type Requirement (v1.1):** `SLP` and `T10M` fields **must be stored as float32** (not float16) in the ancillary cache. The surface pressure computation `Ps()` in `smaccl.c3s_lib` evaluates `log(R*T)` where `R*T = 287.058 × 290 ≈ 83246`, which exceeds the float16 maximum value (65504), causing overflow and NaN propagation. This is enforced via `_MERRA_CACHE_FLOAT32_VARS = {'SLP', 'T10M'}` in the implementation.

## AMIP Ensemble

The AMIP (Atmospheric Model Intercomparison Project) ensemble provides 10 members of monthly aerosol climatology for uncertainty estimation through ensemble spread.

**Source:** MERRA-2 AMIP-10 ensemble reanalysis (`m2amip01.tavgM_2d_aer_Nx.*.nc4`)

**Fields:** Same aerosol fields as MERRA-2, per ensemble member

**Processing:**

- 10 ensemble members are loaded monthly
- Monthly climatology is computed (no temporal interpolation)
- For years 2018-2020, the 2017 climatology is used as fallback
- Each ensemble member provides a complete set of aerosol species for model selection

**Aerosol model selection from AMIP:** For each ensemble member `j`, the closest aerosol model `iaero_AMIPj` is selected using the same Euclidean distance method as for the primary MERRA-2 data (see @sec-aerosol-selection).

## SMAC Coefficients

SMAC coefficients are pre-computed lookup tables that parameterize the radiative transfer model for 148 aerosol models.

**Format:** Pre-computed `.npy` or `.nc` files

**Structure:**

| Dimension | Size | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Aerosol models | 148 | Predefined aerosol mixtures (see Appendix B) |
| Bands | 4 | VIS-NIR (B1-B3) + SWIR (B4) |
| Coefficients | Variable | SMAC polynomial coefficients |

: SMAC coefficient table structure {#tbl-smac-coeffs}

**Coefficient indices:** Stored in `ca_ind` dictionary mapping names (`a0taup`, `a1taup`, `taur`, etc.) to array indices.

**Loading:** `read_smac_coefficients()` supports both `.npy` (structured array) and `.nc` (NetCDF) formats with shape homogenization for broadcast compatibility.

## C3S Surface Albedo / BRDF

The C3S (Copernicus Climate Change Service) Multi-Sensor Surface Albedo product provides BRDF coefficients for non-Lambertian surface correction.

**Source:** C3S L3 BRDF product (RossThick-LiSparse-R model)

**Fields:**

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `k0` | Isotropic albedo coefficient |
| `k1` | Geometric kernel coefficient |
| `k2` | Volumetric kernel coefficient |

: C3S BRDF fields {#tbl-brdf-fields}

**Normalization:** BRDF shape parameters are normalized as:

$$k_{1}^{p} = \frac{k_{1}}{k_{0}} ;\ k_{2}^{p} = \frac{k_{2}}{k_{0}}$$

**Usage:** In the baseline run, the surface is assumed Lambertian (`k1p = k2p = 0`). A second run with BRDF coefficients from C3S allows estimation of the BRDF effect on TOC reflectance.

**Interpolation:** Bilinear interpolation to satellite grid via `load_brdf()`.

# Atmospheric Correction Algorithm

## SMAC Overview

The Simplified Method for Atmospheric Correction (SMAC) approximates the radiative transfer equation with fast polynomial fits pre-computed from a dense sampling of exact RTM (6S/ARTDECO) simulations.

**Core equations:**

The TOA reflectance is modeled as:

$$R_{toa} = T_{dir} \cdot R_{toc} \cdot e^{-\frac{\tau}{\mu_s}} + R_{a} + R_{as} \qquad (1)$$

Where:

- $T_{dir}$: Direct atmospheric transmission
- $R_{toc}$: TOC reflectance (unknown to be solved)
- $\tau$: Total atmospheric optical depth (aerosol + Rayleigh)
- $\mu_s = \cos(\theta_s)$: Cosine of solar zenith angle
- $R_a$: Atmospheric reflectance (Rayleigh scattering only)
- $R_{as}$: Aerosol scattering reflectance

The atmospheric correction inverts this equation:

$$R_{toc} = \frac{R_{toa} - R_{a} - R_{as}}{T_{dir} \cdot e^{-\frac{\tau}{\mu_s}}} \qquad (2)$$

The atmospheric correction factor ($\eta$) is:

$$\eta = \frac{1}{T_{dir} + s_{atm} \cdot R_{toc}} \qquad (3)$$

Where $s_{atm}$ accounts for multiple scattering between atmosphere and surface.

**Gaseous absorption:** SMAC coefficients include pre-fitted gaseous transmission terms for O₃ and H₂O:

$$T_{gas} = \exp\left(-a \cdot (m \cdot U_{gas})^n\right) \qquad (4)$$

Where $m$ is the air mass, $U_{gas}$ is the gas column amount, and $a$, $n$ are SMAC coefficients fitted from line-by-line RT calculations (HITRAN database).

## Sensitivities and Jacobians

The uncertainty propagation requires the sensitivity of $R_{toc}$ to each input parameter (Jacobian matrices).

**Analytical Jacobians:**

| Parameter | Jacobian | Method |
|-----------|----------|--------|
| $R_{toa}$ | $J_{R_{toa}} = \partial R_{toc}/\partial R_{toa}$ | Analytical (Eq. 6-7) |
| $U_{O3}$ | $J_{U_{O3}} = \partial R_{toc}/\partial U_{O3}$ | Analytical (Eq. 8-13) |
| $U_{H2O}$ | $J_{U_{H2O}} = \partial R_{toc}/\partial U_{H2O}$ | Analytical (Eq. 8-13) |

: Analytical Jacobians {#tbl-analytical-jacobians}

**Finite-difference Jacobians:**

| Parameter | Jacobian | Perturbation |
|-----------|----------|-------------|
| $P_s$ | $J_{P_s} = \partial R_{toc}/\partial P_s$ | $\delta P_s$ = 10 hPa |
| $\tau_a^{550}$ | $J_{\tau_a} = \partial R_{toc}/\partial \tau_a$ | $\delta\tau$ = 0.1$\cdot\tau$ |

: Finite-difference Jacobians {#tbl-fd-jacobians}

All Jacobians are computed in the SMAC-CL OpenCL kernel (pixel-parallel execution).

## Atmospheric Transmissions

The total atmospheric transmission is decomposed into:

$$T = T_{dir} + T_{dif} \qquad (5)$$

Where:

- $T_{dir}$: Direct (beam) transmission
- $T_{dif}$: Diffuse (scattered) transmission

**Optical depths:**

Aerosol optical depth (from SMAC coefficients):

$$\tau_a = a_0 + a_1 \cdot AOD_{550} \qquad (6)$$

Rayleigh optical depth (from SMAC coefficients):

$$\tau_r = \tau_{r,coeff} \cdot P \qquad (7)$$

Where $P$ is the equivalent pressure.

# Quality Flags

Quality flags are constructed as a bitfield (int16) with the following bits:

| Bit | Name | Condition | Description |
|-----|------|-----------|-------------|
| 0 | Cloud | `clm != 0` | Cloud-contaminated pixel |
| 1 | High AOD | `TOTEXTTAU >= 0.6` | High aerosol loading |
| 2 | High SZA | `SZA > 80°` | Large solar zenith angle |
| 3 | AOD Gradient | `aod_grad > threshold` | High AOD spatial gradient |
| 4 | AC Failure | `rTOC` is NaN/invalid | Atmospheric correction algorithm failure |
| 5 | Missing Aux | MERRA-2 field is NaN | Missing auxiliary data |
| 6 | Out of LUT | Coefficient bounds exceeded | Out of SMAC lookup table range |

: Quality flag bit definitions {#tbl-quality-flags}

**Flag construction:** `build_flag()` in `funcs.py` implements all 7 flag bits with configurable thresholds.

**AOD gradient:** Computed as `aod_grad = sqrt(gradient_y² + gradient_x²)` using `da.gradient()` (dask gradient).

# Uncertainty Analysis

## Primary Uncertainty Equation

Following the FIDUCEO methodology, the total uncertainty on $R_{toc}$ is computed by propagating all input uncertainties through the measurement equation:

$$u^2(R_{toc}) = \sum_{i=1}^{7} J_i^2 \cdot u^2(x_i) + u^2_{ensemble} + u^2_{BRDF} + u^2_{RTM} + u^2(0) \qquad (19)$$

Where the 7 propagated effects are:

| Effect | Parameter | Jacobian | Uncertainty |
|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| 1 | $R_{toa}$ | $J_{R_{toa}}$ | $u(R_{toa})$ from L1C |
| 2 | $U_{H2O}$ | $J_{U_{H2O}}$ | $u(U_{H2O})$ from MERRA-2 |
| 3 | $U_{O3}$ | $J_{U_{O3}}$ | $u(U_{O3})$ from MERRA-2 |
| 4 | $P_s$ | $J_{P_s}$ | $u(P_s)$ from DEM + meteo |
| 5 | $\tau_a^{550}$ | $J_{\tau_a}$ | $u(\tau_a)$ from validation |
| 6 | Aerosol model | — | Ensemble spread |
| 7 | RTM approximation | — | BRDF + terrain + SMAC fit |

: The 7 propagated uncertainty effects {#tbl-seven-effects}

**Implementation:** `compute_urtoc()` in `process.py` implements Eq. 19 with all 9 terms, returning both total uncertainty and individual component absolute values for diagnostics.

## Uncertainty Effects

### Effect 1: TOA Reflectance Uncertainty

The TOA reflectance uncertainty is derived from the L1C product error components:

$$\sigma(R_{toa}) = \sqrt{u_{random}^2 + u_{structured}^2 + u_{systematic}^2} \qquad (20)$$

Where `UNC_RANDOM`, `UNC_STRUCTURED`, and `UNC_SYSTEMATIC` are band-dependent uncertainty components from the L1C DTOA-r product.

### Effect 2: Ozone Uncertainty

| Parameter | Value | Source |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| Relative uncertainty | 6% of UO₃ | Wargan et al. (2017), Davis et al. (2017) |
| Spatial correlation | ~500 km (synoptic) | Meteorological scale |
| Temporal correlation | ~3 days | Ozone lifetime |

: Ozone uncertainty parameters {#tbl-ozone-unc}

### Effect 3: Water Vapor Uncertainty

| Parameter | Value | Source |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| Relative uncertainty | 20% of UH₂O | Conservative estimate |
| Spatial correlation | ~200 km (convective) | Meteorological scale |
| Temporal correlation | ~1 day | Water vapor lifetime |

: Water vapor uncertainty parameters {#tbl-h2o-unc}

### Effect 4: Surface Pressure Uncertainty

Surface pressure is computed from MERRA-2 sea-level pressure corrected for terrain elevation:

$$P_s = P_0 \cdot \exp\left(-\frac{g \cdot \Delta h}{R \cdot T}\right) \qquad (21)$$

Where:

- $P_0$: Sea-level pressure from MERRA-2 SLP
- $g = 9.80665$ m/s²: Gravitational acceleration
- $R = 287.058$ J/(kg·K): Gas constant for dry air
- $\Delta h = h_{pixel} - h_{MERRA}$: Elevation difference
- $T = T_{10M}$: Temperature at 10m from MERRA-2

The pressure uncertainty combines meteorological and elevation components:

$$u(P_s) = \frac{\sqrt{(\Delta_{P_s}^{\lambda})^2 + (\Delta_{P_s}^{z})^2}}{2} \qquad (22)$$

Where $\Delta_{P_s}^{\lambda} \approx 1$ hPa (meteorological model uncertainty) and $\Delta_{P_s}^{z}$ from DEM elevation uncertainty.

> **Critical Implementation Note (v1.1):** The computation of $P_s$ involves the term $\log(R \cdot T)$ in the `smaccl.c3s_lib.Ps()` function. For typical temperatures $T \approx 290$ K, the product $R \cdot T = 287.058 \times 290 \approx 83246$ exceeds the float16 maximum value (65504), causing overflow and NaN propagation through all downstream computations. **Therefore, `SLP` and `T10M` fields must be stored and processed as float32.** This is enforced in the implementation via `_MERRA_CACHE_FLOAT32_VARS = {'SLP', 'T10M'}`.

### Effect 5: AOD Uncertainty

The AOD uncertainty model is based on comprehensive MERRA-2 validation against AERONET (Gueymard & Yang, 2020):

$$\sigma\left( \tau_{a}^{550} \right) = \max\left( \sigma_{base},\ \sigma_{relative} \times \tau_{a}^{550} \right) \qquad (23)$$

Where:

- $\sigma_{base} = 0.03$ (clean atmosphere baseline)
- $\sigma_{relative} = 0.15$ (15% relative uncertainty)

**Regional multipliers (planned feature):**

| Region Type | Multiplier | Example Locations |
|-------------|------------|-------------------|
| Oceanic/Polar | 0.7× | Open oceans, Arctic |
| North America/Europe | 1.0× | Baseline reference |
| Dust-dominated | 1.5× | Sahara, Arabian Peninsula |
| Biomass burning | 1.4× | Amazon, Sub-Saharan Africa |
| High pollution | 1.3× | East Asia, South Asia |

: Köppen-Geiger regional AOD uncertainty multipliers (planned) {#tbl-kg-multipliers}

> **Implementation Note (v1.1):** Köppen-Geiger climate classification multipliers are documented as a **planned feature** for a future update. The current implementation uses the global model (Eq. 23) without regional multipliers.

### Effect 6: Aerosol Model Uncertainty {#sec-aerosol-selection}

The aerosol model uncertainty is quantified using the AMIP 10-member ensemble spread:

$$u_{ensemble}(R_{toc}) = \text{std}(R_{toc,j}),\ j=1..10 \qquad (24)$$

For each pixel:

1. Select 10 preferred aerosol models from 148 options (based on MERRA-2 species fractions matching AMIP ensemble)
2. Run SMAC with each model → $R_{toc,1} ... R_{toc,10}$
3. $u_{ensemble} = \text{standard\_deviation}(R_{toc,j})$

### Effect 7: RTM Approximation Uncertainty

Three sources of RT approximation errors:

#### 7a. Lambertian Surface Assumption (BRDF Effect)

The BRDF effect is estimated by comparing Lambertian vs BRDF-corrected runs:

$$u_{BRDF}(R_{toc}) = R_{toc}(k_{1p}=0, k_{2p}=0) - R_{toc}(k_{1p} \neq 0, k_{2p} \neq 0) \qquad (25)$$

Significance: Moderate (NIR, high AOD). Magnitude: ~1% for most cases, up to 5% for strong BRDF + high AOD.

#### 7b. Terrain Slope Effects

The CESBIO terrain model computes the terrain-induced relative effect:

$$slope_{eff} = \frac{T}{T_{dir} \cdot \frac{\mu_i}{\mu_s} + T_{dif} \cdot f_{sky} + T \cdot f_{ground} \cdot \rho_{env}} \qquad (26)$$

$$u_{terrain}(R_{toc}) = R_{toc} \cdot (slope_{eff} - 1) \qquad (27)$$

Where:

- $\mu_s = \cos(\theta_s)$: Cosine of solar zenith angle
- $\mu = \cos(s)$: Cosine of terrain slope
- $\mu_i = \mu_s \cdot \mu + \sqrt{1 - \mu_s^2} \cdot \sqrt{1 - \mu^2} \cdot \cos(\phi_s - \phi_a)$: Effective cosine on tilted surface
- $f_{sky} = (1 + \mu)/2$: Sky view factor
- $f_{ground} = (1 - \mu)/2$: Ground view factor
- $\rho_{env}$: Environment reflectance (assumed equal to pixel reflectance)

Significance: Moderate to High in mountainous regions. Magnitude: Several %, up to 40% in extreme terrain.

#### 7c. SMAC Polynomial Fitting Errors

The SMAC fit uncertainty is estimated using an ONNX (Open Neural Network Exchange) model:

$$u_{RTM\_fit}(R_{toc}) = f_{ONNX}(\text{SZA, VZA, RAA, AOD}, P_s, R_{toc}, \text{iaer, wavelength}) \qquad (28)$$

**ONNX model inputs:**

| Input | Description | Units |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| `pression` | Surface pressure | hPa |
| `iaer` | Aerosol model index | 0-147 |
| `raa` | Relative azimuth angle | degrees (0-180) |
| `rtoc` | Surface reflectance | — |
| `sza` | Solar zenith angle | degrees |
| `tauaer` | AOD at 550nm | — |
| `vza` | View zenith angle | degrees |
| `wvl` | Wavelength | nm |

: ONNX RTM fit uncertainty model inputs {#tbl-onnx-inputs}

Mean square error of the ONNX model: ~0.0097 on predicted RTM uncertainty.

## Second-Order Effects

Effects considered minor and not explicitly propagated:

| Effect | Estimated Magnitude | Justification |
|--------|---------------------|---------------|
| Trace gas absorption (CO₂, CH₄, N₂O) | <0.001 | Window channel placement |
| Rayleigh scattering altitude dependence | <0.002 | Accurate DEM + pressure correction |
| LUT interpolation errors | <0.001 | Dense sampling validated vs. 6S |
| Aerosol hygroscopic growth | <0.005 | Implicit in fixed RH=80% model |
| Polarization | <0.003 | Minor for broad-band sensors |
| Spectral band mismatch | <0.002 | SMAC computed for actual RSR |

: Second-order uncertainty effects {#tbl-second-order}

**Combined second-order:** $u(0) \approx 0.007$ (added quadratically to total, configurable via `[Coefficients] u2_0` in config).

## Uncertainty Characterization Summary

### Input Uncertainty Estimates

| Input Parameter | Source | Uncertainty Estimate | Notes |
|-----------------|--------|---------------------|-------|
| $R_{toa}$ | Level 1C FDR | From DTOA-r | Band-dependent, separated into random/structured/common |
| $U_{O3}$ | MERRA-2 + literature | 6% | Wargan et al. (2017), Davis et al. (2017) |
| $U_{H2O}$ | MERRA-2 + estimate | 20% | Conservative, needs validation |
| $P_0$ | MERRA-2 SLP | <1 hPa | Meteorological model uncertainty |
| $\lambda$ (lapse rate) | Model assumption | 0.002 K/m | Van Besselaar et al. (2011) |
| $z$ (elevation) | GTOPO30 + geoloc | $\Delta_{elev}$ (DEM stddev) | Convolution with $\sigma=0.5$ pixels (~0.3 VGT pixel) |
| $\tau_a^{550}$ | MERRA-2 + validation | max(0.03, 0.15×$\tau_a$) | Gueymard & Yang (2020), regionalization planned |
| Aerosol model | AMIP ensemble | Ensemble spread | 10-member AMIP runs |
| RTM approximations | Modelling | 1-30% | Scene dependent. Worst case: rough terrain, high AOD |

: Input uncertainty estimates summary {#tbl-input-unc-summary}

### Correlation Structures

| Effect | Spatial Correlation | Temporal Correlation | Implementation |
|--------|---------------------|---------------------|----------------|
| TOA-r (random) | None | None | Average as $\sqrt{\Sigma u^2}/N$ |
| TOA-r (structured) | Scan-line or column | None | Use L1C correlation matrix |
| TOA-r (common) | Global | Global | Simple average (no reduction) |
| $U_{O3}$ | ~500 km (synoptic) | ~3 days | Exponential decay |
| $U_{H2O}$ | ~200 km (convective) | ~1 day | Exponential decay |
| $P_s$ (meteorological) | Global | >1 month | Common (no reduction) |
| $P_s$ (elevation) | DEM resolution | Permanent | Structured (terrain-correlated) |
| $\tau_a^{550}$ | ~200-500 km (plume) | ~1 week | Exponential decay |
| Aerosol model | 1° (climatology grid) | 1 week | Rectangle function |
| RT approximations | Scene-dependent | None | Random or systematic (case-by-case) |

: Correlation structures for propagated uncertainty effects {#tbl-correlation-structures}

## Limitations and Future Improvements

### Known Limitations

| Limitation | Impact | Status |
|------------|--------|--------|
| Surface BRDF: Simplified kernel model | Up to 10% error in directional reflectance over non-forest surfaces | Documented |
| Terrain effect: Simplified model | Several % in mountainous regions | Documented |
| Water Vapor Uncertainty: Conservative 20% estimate | May overestimate total uncertainty in dry conditions | Future: Refined characterization from MERRA-2 validation |
| Adjacency Effects: Not corrected | Significant for high-contrast scenes (coast, cloud edge) | Known limitation — no correction applied |
| Köppen-Geiger regionalization | Regional AOD uncertainty multipliers not applied | Planned feature — data and multipliers defined but not implemented |

: Known limitations and future improvements {#tbl-known-limitations}

# Conclusions

This ATBD demonstrates a comprehensive implementation of the FIDUCEO uncertainty propagation methodology for atmospheric correction of satellite vegetation sensors. Key achievements:

- **Rigorous Uncertainty Quantification**: All major uncertainty sources are identified, characterized, and propagated through the measurement equation
- **Operational AOD Uncertainty Model**: Based on extensive MERRA-2 validation (Gueymard & Yang 2020), providing realistic AOD uncertainties: $\sigma(\tau_a) = \max(0.03, 0.15 \times \tau_a)$ with regional adjustments planned
- **Ensemble Approach for Model Uncertainty**: AMIP 10-member ensemble quantifies aerosol optical property uncertainties
- **Efficient GPU Implementation**: SMAC-CL enables fast processing while computing full Jacobian matrices
- **Validation Framework**: Following Aerosol_cci and FIDUCEO best practices (Popp & Mittaz 2022)

This approach provides climate-quality TOC reflectances with fully traceable, metrologically sound uncertainties suitable for:

- Long-term surface monitoring
- Multi-sensor harmonization
- Climate model validation

{{< pagebreak >}}

# References

**FIDUCEO Methodology:**

- Mittaz, J., Merchant, C.J., Woolliams, E.R. (2019). Applying principles of metrology to historical Earth observations from satellites. *Metrologia*, 56, 032002.
- Popp, T., Mittaz, J. (2022). Systematic Propagation of AVHRR AOD Uncertainties — A Case Study to Demonstrate the FIDUCEO Approach. *Remote Sensing*, 14(4), 875.

**MERRA-2 Validation:**

- Gueymard, C., Yang, D. (2020). Worldwide validation of CAMS and MERRA-2 reanalysis aerosol optical depth products using 15 years of AERONET observations. *Atmospheric Environment*, 225, 117216.
- Wargan, K., et al. (2017). Evaluation of the Ozone Fields in NASA's MERRA-2 Reanalysis. *Journal of Climate*, 30, 2961-2988.
- Davis, S.M., et al. (2017). Assessment of upper tropospheric and stratospheric water vapor and ozone in reanalyses. *Atmospheric Chemistry and Physics*, 17, 12743-12778.

**Atmospheric Correction:**

- Rahman, H., Dedieu, G. (1994). SMAC: A simplified method for the atmospheric correction of satellite measurements in the solar spectrum. *International Journal of Remote Sensing*, 15, 123-143.
- Vermote, E.F., et al. (1997). Second Simulation of the Satellite Signal in the Solar Spectrum (6S). *IEEE TGRS*, 35, 675-686.

**Aerosol Climatology:**

- Kinne, S., et al. (2006). An AeroCom initial assessment — optical properties in aerosol component modules of global models. *Atmospheric Chemistry and Physics*, 6, 1815-1834.

**Surface Pressure:**

- Van Besselaar, E.J.M., et al. (2011). A European daily high-resolution observational gridded data set of sea level pressure. *Journal of Geophysical Research*, 116, D11110.
- Breon, F.-M., A. Vermeulen and J. Descloitres, An evaluation of satellite aerosol products against sunphotometer measurements, Remote Sensing of Environment, Volume 115, Issue 12, pp 3102-3111, 2011.
- Buchard, V., et al. The MERRA-2 Aerosol Reanalysis, 1980 Onward. Part II: Evaluation and Case Studies. J. Climate, 30, 6851-6872, 2017.
- Ramon D. and R. Santer, Operational remote sensing of aerosols over land to account for directional effects, Applied Optics-LP, 40, 3060-3075 (2001).
- Randles, C.A., et al. The MERRA-2 Aerosol Reanalysis, 1980 Onward. Part I: System Description and Data Assimilation Evaluation. Journal of Climate 30:17, 6823-6850, 2017.

**RTM and Spectroscopy:**

- Schreier, F., et al. (2019) Py4CAtS — PYthon for Computational ATmospheric Spectroscopy. *Atmosphere*, 10, 262.
- Gordon, I.E., et al. (2017) The HITRAN2016 molecular spectroscopic database. *J. Quant. Spectrosc. Radiat. Transfer* 203, 3-69.
- Kochanov, R.V., et al. (2016). HITRAN Application Programming Interface (HAPI). *J. Quant. Spectrosc. Radiat. Transfer,* 177, 15-30.
- Hess, M.; Koepke, P. & Schult, I, (1998). Optical Properties of Aerosols and Clouds: The Software Package OPAC. *Bulletin of the American Meteorological Society*, 79, 831-844.

{{< pagebreak >}}

# Appendix A: SMAC Coefficient Calculation

## Spectral Response Functions and Gaseous Absorption

Radiative transfer computations are done for all camera RSRFs (CENTER, LEFT, RIGHT). Typical atmospheric gaseous transmissions are computed for each Proba-V spectral band.

## Processing Chain

SMAC coefficients are derived from basic data through:

1. **Spectroscopic data** (HITRAN 2012/2016) → Py4Cats/HAPI → line-by-line radiative transfer
2. **Gaseous absorption regression** → SMAC coefficients for O₃ and H₂O transmission fits
3. **ARTDECO RT simulations** → TOA reflectance for dense geometry/pressure/AOD sampling
4. **Multi-dimensional polynomial fitting** → SMAC scattering coefficients
5. **Fit accuracy validation** → RMSE vs 6S, neural network verification

## Gaseous Absorption Modelling

With the RSRF data, typical absorption is computed based on the HITRAN 2012/2016 database and Py4Cats/HAPI. Regression of gaseous transmission versus gas concentrations, air masses, and barometric pressure yields SMAC coefficients:

$$T = \exp\left(a \cdot (m \cdot U_{H2O})^n\right)$$

Where $m$ is the air mass and $U_{H2O}$ is the water vapor column.

## Radiative Transfer Computations and Fit Accuracy

Radiative transfer equation for each pixel (geometry, band, AOT, $P_{surf}$) is solved using ARTDECO RT package. TOA reflectance computations are done for a black surface and no gaseous absorption:

- **RAA:** 121 values from 0 to 180°
- **SZA:** 38 values from 0 to 89° (used only if <70°)
- **VZA:** 47 values from 0 to 89° (used only if <70°)
- **Surface pressure:** 6 values from 600 to 1050 hPa
- **AOT at 550 nm:** 16 values from 0.01 to 1.5 (used only if < 0.8)

Several multi-dimensional fits are performed to give the SMAC model parameters. Fit accuracy is validated against exact ARTDECO computations.

## Fit Uncertainty Model (ONNX)

A lightweight Feed-Forward Neural Network (MLP) in ONNX format estimates SMAC fit uncertainty.

**Inputs:** Angles (SZA, VZA, RAA), AOD at 550 nm, pressure, TOC-r, iaer, TOA-r, wavelength

**Performance:** Mean Square Error of 0.0097 on predicted RTM uncertainty on $R_{toc}$.

**Runtime:** `onnxruntime.InferenceSession` loaded once per worker process.

## BRDF Effect Estimation

The baseline AC is run under the assumption of a Lambertian surface. If the surface BRDF shape is known, the RossThick-LiSparse-R model is used:

$$\rho_g(\theta_s, \theta_v, \phi) = k_0 + k_1 F_1(\theta_s, \theta_v, \phi) + k_2 F_2(\theta_s, \theta_v, \phi) \qquad (25)$$

The fast RTM with BRDF uses the 6S formulation with modified total atmospheric transmission $T_{atm,g}$ (Ramon and Santer, 2001). The 12 regression coefficients $d_i^k$ and $b_i^k$ for kernel directional averages are stored in the SMAC coefficient structure.

# Appendix B: Aerosol Model Definitions

## Aerosol Variability on Earth

44,688 MERRA-2 grid points over land. Four dates represent seasonal variability: 2011/01/01, 2011/04/01, 2011/07/01, and 2011/10/01 at 13:00.

Aerosol models are defined according to MERRA-2 5 AOT ratios:

$$X_u = \frac{\tau_{a;u}^{550}}{\tau_a^{550}},\ u \in \{DU, SU, OC, BC, SS\}$$

Each mixing ratio $X_u$ covers 0-100% range (except BC < 25%). From 5×5×5×5×6=3750 possible combinations, 148 combinations effectively occur on the 44,688 pixels of 2011/07/01.

## Aerosol Optical Properties

MERRA-2 aerosol components are mapped to OPAC models (RH=80%):

| MERRA-2 Component | OPAC Model/Component |
|-------------------|---------------------|
| SU/SU | Antarctic model |
| DU/DU | Desert model |
| OC/OM | Water soluble (waso) |
| SS/SS | Maritime clean |
| BC/BC | Soot |

: MERRA-2 to OPAC aerosol component mapping {#tbl-opac-mapping}

**Spectral dependence:** $K(\lambda) = \sum_u K_u(\lambda) X_u$

**Single scattering albedo:** $\varpi_0(\lambda) = \sum_u \varpi_0^u(\lambda) X_u$

**Phase function:** $P(\theta,\lambda) = \sum_u P_u(\theta,\lambda) X_u$

**Asymmetry parameter:** $g(\lambda) = \frac{1}{2} \int_{-1}^{1} \mu P(\mu,\lambda) d\mu$

# Appendix C: SMAC-CL Implementation

## Input Reading

For each pixel, the following quantities are read from Level 1 files:

- TOA radiance: `TOA-r(λ)`
- Error: `DTOA-r(λ)`
- Solar extra-terrestrial irradiance: `Es(λ)`
- Geometry: SZA, SAA, VZA, VAA, VZA_SWIR, VAA_SWIR
- Location: lat, lon

Sensor name, date, and mean acquisition time (UTC) are stored as file attributes for SMAC coefficient and MERRA-2 file selection.

## Pixel Masking

Cloud-contaminated pixels are masked (`clm != 0`). Pixels with SZA > 90° are also excluded.

## Spatial and Temporal Interpolation

- **DEM:** Linear interpolation (improvement over nearest-neighbor in v1.0)
- **MERRA-2:** `RegularGridInterpolator(method='linear')` — bilinear spatial + linear temporal
- **AMIP:** Spatial interpolation only (monthly climatology, no temporal interpolation)

**Unit conversions:**

| Variable | MERRA-2 Unit | SMAC Unit | Conversion |
|----------|-------------|-----------|------------|
| $U_{O3}$ | Dobson | cm.atm | 1e-3 |
| $U_{H2O}$ | kg.m⁻² | g.cm⁻² | 1e-1 |
| $P_0$ | Pa | hPa | 1e-2 |

: Unit conversions applied in SMAC-CL {#tbl-unit-conversions}

Surface pressure $P_s$ and uncertainty $\Delta P_s$ are computed using Eqs. (21)-(24).

## Aerosol Model Selection

$$\text{iaero} = \text{argmin}_i \sum_u (X_u - X_{u,i})^2,\ i=0..147$$

The $X_{u,i}$ basis for 148 predefined models is stored in `Aerosol_model_fraction.txt` and read once.

## SMAC-CL Function

**Architecture:**

- Language: OpenCL (compatible with GPU and CPU)
- Interface: Python wrapper (`smaccl` library)
- Processing mode: Pixel-parallel (massively parallel on GPU)

**Main Processing Function:**

```python
def smac_cl_run(
    coeffs,           # SMAC coefficients (148 aerosol models)
    tetas, tetav,     # SZA, VZA arrays
    phis, phiv,       # SAA, VAA arrays
    uh2o, uo3,        # Water vapor, ozone columns
    taup550,          # AOD at 550 nm
    pressure,         # Surface pressure
    rtoa,             # TOA reflectance
    k1p, k2p,         # BRDF kernel ratios (default 0 for Lambertian)
    iaero_list        # List of aerosol model indices (11 models)
):
    """
    Inputs: All arrays dimensioned [npixels] or [nbands, npixels]
    Outputs:
      - rtoc: TOC reflectance [nbands, npixels]
      - jacobians: Sensitivity matrices
      - u_ensemble: Std dev from aerosol ensemble
    """
```

**Processing Flow:**

```
1. Reorganize input arrays for GPU efficiency (transpose, flatten, pad)
2. Upload data to GPU/CPU memory
   - TOA reflectances, geometry, auxiliary data
   - SMAC coefficients (pre-loaded once)
3. GPU/CPU kernel execution (per-pixel parallel)
   a. Compute gaseous transmissions
   b. Compute Rayleigh + aerosol scattering
   c. Apply SMAC equations -> Rtoc
   d. Compute analytical Jacobians
   e. Finite-difference Jacobians (Ps, tau550)
   f. 10 AMIP ensemble members
4. Download results from GPU/CPU (Rtoc, Jacobians, ensemble statistics)
5. CPU post-processing
   - Combine uncertainty components
   - Apply terrain corrections
   - Quality flag assignment
```

**Performance:**

- GPU acceleration: ~100× faster than CPU for large images
- Memory requirement: ~2 GB GPU RAM for full-swath processing
- Typical processing time: <1 second per tile (1000×1000 pixels)

## Level 2A Output Variables

| Output | Description | Dimensions | Dtype |
|--------|-------------|------------|-------|
| `rTOC` | TOC directional reflectance | (bands, height, width) | float32 |
| `UrTOC` | Total uncertainty | (bands, height, width) | float32 |
| `Jrtoa` | Jacobian vs Rtoa | (bands, height, width) | float32 |
| `Juo3` | Jacobian vs UO3 | (bands, height, width) | float32 |
| `Juh2o` | Jacobian vs UH2O | (bands, height, width) | float32 |
| `Jpre` | Jacobian vs Ps | (bands, height, width) | float32 |
| `Jtau550` | Jacobian vs AOD | (bands, height, width) | float32 |
| `flag` | Quality flags | (height, width) | int16 |
| `iaero` | Aerosol model index | (height, width) | int32 |
| `TOTEXTTAU` | AOD 550 used | (height, width) | float32 |
| `SZA`, `SAA`, `VZA`, `VAA` | Geometry | (height, width) | float32 |
| `clm` | Cloud mask | (height, width) | uint8 |
| `SM_MAP_B1..B4` | Status maps | (height, width) | uint8 |

: Level 2A standard output variables {#tbl-l2a-outputs}

**Diagnostic outputs (additional):**

| Output | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `unc_h2o` | H₂O uncertainty component |
| `unc_o3` | O₃ uncertainty component |
| `unc_ps` | Pressure uncertainty component |
| `unc_aot` | AOD uncertainty component |
| `slope_err` | Terrain slope error |
| `urtoc_terrain` | Terrain uncertainty contribution |
| `UrTOC_rtm_brdf` | BRDF uncertainty contribution |
| `UrTOC_rtm_fit` | RTM fit uncertainty contribution |

: Level 2A diagnostic output variables {#tbl-l2a-diagnostics}

# Appendix D: Implementation Notes

This appendix documents implementation-specific details, performance optimizations, and known limitations of the FDR4VGT production codebase. These are not changes to the scientific algorithm but rather engineering decisions for operational efficiency.

## Performance Optimizations {#sec-perf-opt}

### Ancillary Zarr Cache

| Aspect | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **What** | Pre-interpolates MERRA2 fields to full satellite grid once, stores in local Zarr cache |
| **Why** | MERRA2 source files on network FS; per-tile interpolation re-reads network and repeats work |
| **Function** | `precompute_ancillary()` in `process.py` |
| **Toggle** | `[Sizes] anc_cache` (default on) |
| **Numerical equivalence** | Pointwise interpolation → identical to per-tile interpolation (halo reads from cache) |
| **Memory** | Streamed tile-by-tile to bound peak memory (~2.8 GB → safe on 4 GB nodes) |

: Ancillary Zarr cache optimization {#tbl-zarr-cache}

**Cache format:** Zarr format 2, most fields as float16 (small values, sufficient precision), `SLP` and `T10M` as float32 (overflow protection).

### Tile-Based Processing

| Aspect | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **What** | Processes image in overlapping tiles (`batch_size` × `batch_size` with halo) |
| **Why** | Bounded memory per tile, parallel processing with workers |
| **Function** | `process_batched()` generates tile grid, `_worker_compute_tile()` processes each |
| **Config** | `[Sizes] chunks_size` (default 512), `[Sizes] nworkers` (default 1) |
| **Halo** | ±1 pixel halo for terrain slope/aspect computation (edge effects) |

: Tile-based processing optimization {#tbl-tile-processing}

### Local Output File

| Aspect | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **What** | Writes output NetCDF to local temp file, moves to final destination once at end |
| **Why** | Serial per-tile append writes degrade on network FS (ceph) — 2s → 5s/tile |
| **Function** | `process_batched()` creates temp file, `shutil.move()` at end |
| **Toggle** | `[Sizes] local_output` (default on) |

: Local output file optimization {#tbl-local-output}

### Single NetCDF Handle

| Aspect | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **What** | Opens output NetCDF handle once, reuses for all tiles |
| **Why** | Per-tile open/close/flush was dominant writer cost (HDF5 overhead) |
| **Function** | `nc_out = Dataset(output_path, 'a')` reused across tiles |
| **Flush** | Periodic close+reopen every `flush_interval` tiles to free HDF5 cache |

: Single NetCDF handle optimization {#tbl-single-handle}

### DEM Cropping

| Aspect | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **What** | Crops global GTOPO30 DEM to product's lat/lon extent before loading |
| **Why** | Full DEM ~1.9 GB, cropped ~0.2 GB (narrow lon range) |
| **Function** | `xrcrop(dem_ds, lat=data['y'], lon=data['x'])` |

: DEM cropping optimization {#tbl-dem-cropping}

### Copy-on-Write Shared State

| Aspect | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **What** | Pre-forks worker pool, then populates `_SHARED` dict (inherited via COW) |
| **Why** | Avoids pickling large objects (SMAC coefficients, DEM, MERRA2) per task |
| **Function** | `_SHARED.update()` before pool creation |
| **Objects** | SMAC coefficients (~100 MB), aerosol model LUT, terrain data |

: Copy-on-write shared state optimization {#tbl-cow-shared-state}

## Data Type Requirements

### Float32 for SLP/T10M (Overflow Fix)

| Aspect | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Issue** | Ancillary Zarr cache stored `T10M` and `SLP` as float16 |
| **Root cause** | `smaccl.c3s_lib.Ps()` computes `log(R*T)` where `R*T = 287.058 × 290 ≈ 83246` |
| **Overflow** | 83246 exceeds float16 max (65504) → NaN in pressure correction → NaN rTOC |
| **Fix** | `_MERRA_CACHE_FLOAT32_VARS = {'SLP', 'T10M'}` keeps these fields as float32 |
| **Commit** | `51f7e68` — "Fix ancillary cache dtype overflow causing NaN rTOC" |

: Float32 overflow fix for SLP/T10M {#tbl-float32-fix}

**Validation:** Full processing run (100 tiles, 241.9s) shows rTOC finite on land ~77.2%, ocean ~0% (expected — ocean pixels masked by cloud/quality flags).

### Float16 for Small-Value Fields

Fields with small values (~0-0.1 range) are safely stored as float16:

- `ERROR` (uncertainty components)
- `TOTEXTTAU` (AOD values, typically <1.0)
- Species fractions (0-1 range)

These are widened to float32 on NetCDF write (netCDF4 has no native half-float type).

## Known Limitations

### Adjacency Effects

**Status:** Not corrected.

**Impact:** Significant for high-contrast scenes (coast, cloud edge, urban/rural boundaries). The SMAC algorithm assumes a horizontally homogeneous scene within the pixel footprint. Adjacent pixels with different reflectances contribute to the measured TOA radiance through atmospheric scattering, which is not accounted for.

**Future work:** Implement semi-empirical adjacency correction (e.g., based on scene contrast and atmospheric conditions).

### Köppen-Geiger Regionalization

**Status:** Planned feature.

**Description:** The AOD uncertainty model (Eq. 23) uses global multipliers. Regional multipliers based on Köppen-Geiger climate classification are defined but not yet implemented (see @tbl-kg-multipliers).

**Data available:** `kg_zoneNumGueymard.csv` in ANCILLARY directory.

### Water Vapor Uncertainty

**Status:** Conservative 20% estimate.

**Impact:** May overestimate total uncertainty in dry conditions.

**Future work:** Refined characterization from MERRA-2 validation studies.

## Tile Processing Architecture

### Overview

The processing pipeline uses a tile-based architecture for memory-bounded, parallel execution:

```
process_batched() -- Main entry point
 1. Generate tile grid from input extent
    - Tile size: chunks_size x chunks_size
    - Overlap: +/-1 pixel halo
    - Total tiles: ceil(y/chunks) x ceil(x/chunks)
 2. Precompute ancillary cache (if enabled)
    - Interpolate MERRA2 to full grid
    - Save to local Zarr cache
 3. Create worker pool (nworkers)
    - Fork workers before populating _SHARED
    - COW inheritance of large objects
 4. Process tiles in parallel
    - _worker_compute_tile() per tile
    - SMAC-CL run (VNIR + SWIR)
    - Uncertainty propagation
    - Write tile to NetCDF
 5. Move local output to final destination
```

### Worker Function

`_worker_compute_tile()` performs end-to-end tile computation:

1. **Read tile data** from input L1C and ancillary cache
2. **Interpolate DEM** and compute terrain (slope/aspect)
3. **Select aerosol model** (closest in 5D species space)
4. **Run SMAC-CL** (VNIR bands, then SWIR band)
5. **Compute BRDF effect** (Lambertian vs BRDF run, if enabled)
6. **Compute terrain effect** (CESBIO model)
7. **Compute RTM fit uncertainty** (ONNX model)
8. **Propagate uncertainties** (`compute_urtoc()`)
9. **Build quality flags** (`build_flag()`)
10. **Write tile** to output NetCDF

## Output Format Details

### NetCDF Structure

The output NetCDF file follows CF-1.8 conventions with attributes from `cf_attributes_res2.json`.

**Dimensions:**

| Dimension | Size | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `bands` | 4 | B1 (Blue), B2 (Red), B3 (NIR), B4 (SWIR) |
| `height` | Variable | Tile height (y dimension) |
| `width` | Variable | Tile width (x dimension) |

: Output NetCDF dimensions {#tbl-nc-dimensions}

**Global attributes:**

| Attribute | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `sensor` | Sensor name (Proba-V, SPOT-VGT) |
| `date` | Acquisition date |
| `time` | Mean acquisition time (UTC) |
| `projection` | Projection attributes from input L1C |
| `conventions` | CF-1.8 |
| `history` | Processing timestamp and version |

: Output NetCDF global attributes {#tbl-nc-global-attrs}

**Variable attributes:** Each variable has `standard_name`, `units`, `long_name`, and `comments` attributes applied from the CF JSON configuration.

### Chunk Cache Optimization

The output NetCDF is written with optimized chunk sizes matching the tile processing grid, ensuring efficient random access for downstream consumers.

# Appendix E: SMACCL OpenCL Kernel API

This appendix documents the SMACCL OpenCL function API as implemented in the HYGEOS `smaccl` repository (OpenCL branch), including both:

- Python host wrapper API (`smaccl/smaccl.py`, class `Smaccl`)
- OpenCL kernel API (`smaccl/devicecl.cl`, kernel `smaccl`)

The objective is to provide a precise implementation-facing contract for integration and maintenance.

## Python Host API

### Signature

```python
def run(
    self,
    coeffs,
    tetas,
    tetav,
    phis,
    phiv,
    uh2o,
    uo3,
    taup550,
    pression,
    rtoa,
    k1p,
    k2p,
    iaero,
    NBLOOP=1,
)
```

### Returned tuple

```python
(rsurf, rsurf_0, dev_std, Jrtoa, Juo3, Juh2o, Jpre, Jtaup)
```

All returned arrays are `float32` and shaped like `rtoa`.

### Input semantics

- `coeffs`: SMAC coefficients. First dimension defines number of spectral bands processed.
- `tetas`: solar zenith angle (SZA), float32.
- `tetav`: view zenith angle (VZA), float32.
- `phis`: solar azimuth angle (SAA), float32.
- `phiv`: view azimuth angle (VAA), float32.
- `uh2o`: total water vapor column, float32.
- `uo3`: total ozone column, float32.
- `taup550`: aerosol optical depth at 550 nm, float32.
- `pression`: surface pressure, float32.
- `rtoa`: TOA reflectance, float32.
- `k1p`, `k2p`: BRDF normalized kernel ratios. Set to 0 for Lambertian processing.
- `iaero`: aerosol model indices per pixel, with `Naero` candidates.
- `NBLOOP`: number of internal per-thread loops (used for repeated runs/MC patterns).

### Shape contract

The wrapper supports 4D tensor layout for tiled processing:

- `rtoa`: `(NBAND, NZ, XBLOCK, XGRID)`
- Angular and ancillary scalar fields (`tetas`, `tetav`, `phis`, `phiv`, `uh2o`, `uo3`, `taup550`, `pression`): `(NZ, XBLOCK, XGRID)`
- BRDF fields (`k1p`, `k2p`): `(NBAND, NZ, XBLOCK, XGRID)`
- Aerosol index tensor (`iaero`): `(Naero, NZ, XBLOCK, XGRID)`

Where:

- `NBAND`: number of spectral bands handled in current call
- `NZ`: padded depth along pixel index dimension
- `XBLOCK`, `XGRID`: OpenCL launch decomposition
- `Naero`: number of aerosol candidates used in ensemble/selection

### Runtime behavior

1. Allocate output buffers for `rsurf`, `rsurf_0`, `dev_std`, Jacobians.
2. Copy host arrays to OpenCL buffers.
3. Launch OpenCL kernel `smaccl`.
4. Copy outputs from device to host arrays.

## OpenCL Kernel API

### Signature

```c
__kernel void smaccl(
    __global coef_atmos *ca,
    __global float *tetas_,
    __global float *tetav_,
    __global float *phis_,
    __global float *phiv_,
    __global float *uh2o_,
    __global float *uo3_,
    __global float *taup550_,
    __global float *pression_,
    __global float *rtoa_,
    __global float *rsurf,
    __global float *Jr,
    __global float *Juo3,
    __global float *Juh2o,
    __global float *Jpre,
    __global float *Jtaup,
    __global float *k1p_,
    __global float *k2p_,
    __global int *iaero,
    int NMOD,
    int NBLOOPd,
    int NBANDd,
    int NZd
)
```

### Kernel-level constants and finite differences

The implementation uses:

- `dpre = 10.0` hPa for pressure Jacobian finite difference
- `dtau_rel = 0.1` for relative AOD perturbation
- `NRUN = 3 + NBLOOPd`

Interpretation of `NRUN`:

- 1 reference run
- 2 perturbation runs for finite-difference Jacobians (`P_s`, `tau550`)
- `NBLOOPd` optional additional looped runs

## Output Mapping

The host API output tuple maps to AC physics as:

| Host variable | Physical meaning |
|---------------|-------------------|
| `rsurf` | TOC directional reflectance (`rTOC`) |
| `rsurf_0` | Lambertian-equivalent TOC reflectance (`rTOC_0`) |
| `dev_std` | Aerosol-model ensemble spread (`UrTOC_ens`) |
| `Jrtoa` | Jacobian $\partial R_{toc}/\partial R_{toa}$ |
| `Juo3` | Jacobian $\partial R_{toc}/\partial U_{O3}$ |
| `Juh2o` | Jacobian $\partial R_{toc}/\partial U_{H2O}$ |
| `Jpre` | Jacobian $\partial R_{toc}/\partial P_s$ |
| `Jtaup` | Jacobian $\partial R_{toc}/\partial \tau_{550}$ |

: SMACCL kernel output variable mapping {#tbl-kernel-output-mapping}

## ISmaccl Wrapper API

The integration layer `ISmaccl` wraps the kernel API for product-level execution.

### Constructor

```python
ISmaccl(
    config,
    frac_aer_model,
    ca,
    XBLOCK=128,
    XGRID=128,
    breakpoint=False,
    ancillary=False,
    platform=...,  # forwarded to Smaccl(platform=...)
)
```

### Product-level methods

- `run_block(l2_data, batch_size=512)`: block mapping over xarray/dask chunks.
- `run(l2_data, iaero)`: scene/tile execution and assembly of xarray output dataset.
- `exec_smaccl(...)`: low-level preparation/padding/reshaping, kernel call, post-mapping.

### `exec_smaccl` returned tuple

```python
(
    rsurf,
    rsurf0,
    dev_std,
    Juh2o,
    Juo3,
    Jrtoa,
    Jpre,
    Jtaup,
    Duh2o,
    Duo3,
    Drtoa,
    Dpre,
    Dtaup,
)
```

This extends kernel outputs by including uncertainty-input terms (`D*`) computed on host side for subsequent propagation.

### Band handling in Proba-V mode

For Proba-V processing, the wrapper executes two calls:

1. VIS/NIR group using `VZA`/`VAA`
2. SWIR group using `VZA_IR`/`VAA_IR`

Then it merges outputs into full 4-band products.

## Device and Data Contracts

### Data type and ordering requirements

- Arrays converted to C-order contiguous layout
- Floating inputs cast to `float32`
- Aerosol index array cast to integer (`int16`/`int32` depending stage)
- Invalid geolocation values sanitized before kernel packing
- Padded extended arrays (`GSIZEXT`) filled with NaN then reshaped to launch geometry

### Device and queue selection

`Smaccl.__init__` compiles kernel source and creates queue/context via `set_queue(xpu, environment)`.

Supported environment mapping in repository implementation includes:

| Environment | CPU device name | GPU device name |
|-------------|-----------------|------------------|
| `Default` | Portable Computing Language | NVIDIA CUDA |
| `Turenne` | Intel(R) OpenCL | — |
| `TerraScope` | Intel(R) OpenCL | NVIDIA CUDA |

: OpenCL device/environment mapping {#tbl-device-mapping}

If no compatible/available device is found, initialization fails.

### Contractual outputs for FDR4VGT

For this ATBD implementation, the contractual kernel-facing outputs are:

- Reflectance: `rTOC`, `rTOC_0`
- Jacobians: `Jrtoa`, `Juo3`, `Juh2o`, `Jpre`, `Jtau550`
- Aerosol spread: `UrTOC_ens`

Host-side uncertainty terms (`Duh2o`, `Duo3`, `Drtoa`, `Dpre`, `Dtaup`) are combined with Jacobians in the uncertainty propagation equation documented in the Uncertainty Analysis section.

This appendix defines the implementation API used by the operational chain and should be updated if the upstream HYGEOS `smaccl` API changes.
